2603.22593.title: Language Models Can Explain Visual Features via Steering
2603.22593.paper_summary: Sparse Autoencoders uncover thousands of features in vision models, yet explaining these features without requiring human intervention remains an open challenge. While previous work has proposed generating correlation-based explanations based on top activating input examples, we present a fundamentally different alternative based on causal interventions. We leverage the structure of Vision-Language Models and steer individual SAE features in the vision encoder after providing an empty image. Then, we prompt the language model to explain what it ``sees'', effectively eliciting the visual concept represented by each feature. Results show that Steering offers an scalable alternative that complements traditional approaches based on input examples, serving as a new axis for automated interpretability in vision models. Moreover, the quality of explanations improves consistently with the scale of the language model, highlighting our method as a promising direction for future research. Finally, we propose Steering-informed Top-k, a hybrid approach that combines the strengths of causal interventions and input-based approaches to achieve state-of-the-art explanation quality without additional computational cost.
2603.22593.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏自編碼器在視覺模型中揭示了數千個特徵，但在不需要人類介入的情況下解釋這些特徵仍然是一個未解的挑戰。雖然先前的研究提出了基於頂部激活輸入範例生成相關性解釋的方法，但我們提出了一種基於因果干預的根本不同的替代方案。我們利用視覺-語言模型的結構，在提供空白圖像後，引導視覺編碼器中的個別 SAE 特徵。然後，我們提示語言模型解釋它所“看到”的內容，有效地引出每個特徵所代表的視覺概念。結果顯示，Steering 提供了一種可擴展的替代方案，補充了基於輸入範例的傳統方法，成為視覺模型自動可解釋性的新軸心。此外，解釋的質量隨著語言模型的規模穩定提高，突顯了我們的方法作為未來研究的有前景方向。最後，我們提出了基於 Steering 的 Top-k，一種混合方法，結合了因果干預和基於輸入的方法的優勢，以在不增加計算成本的情況下實現最先進的解釋質量。
2603.22593.author: Javier Ferrando et.al.
2603.22593.authors: Javier Ferrando, Enrique Lopez-Cuena, Pablo Agustin Martin-Torres, Daniel Hinjos, Anna Arias-Duart, Dario Garcia-Gasulla
